text: The surgeon uses the FlexDex instrument to perform an anastomosis between the healthy esophagus, visible on the right side of the monitor, and the gastric conduit, visible on the left side, which has been brought into the chest.

The surgical instruments, including the FlexDex, are visibly shaking due to tremor during the thoracic part of the esophagectomy. The thoracic ports appear to be floating, as they are not snugly fitted and there is no pneumoperitoneum used.

The surgeon uses a flexible tip needle holder to catch the tail end of the suture, demonstrating its effectiveness in allowing suturing in various directions. The needle holder's flexible tip is visible, though it exhibits slight tremor and reduced strength compared to a rigid needle holder.
